revision.text:
[[ja:段尻巻古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Danjirimaki Kofun |image = File:Danjirimaki 1.jpg |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Toki|qid=Q847332|1=Toki, Gifu}} |coordinates = 35|21|56.62 |coordinates = 137|10|48.98 |iso_code = JP-21 |type = [[Circular tumulus]] |size = Diameter: 23.9 m
Height: 4.1 m |built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |burial = Pseudo-double-sleeved {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |imperial_tomb = |special_notes = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Circular tumulus |map_dot_label = Danjirimaki Kofun |map_dot_label = top |part_of = {{ill|Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun|qid=Q60769827|1=Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun}} }} '''Danjirimaki Kofun''' (段尻巻古墳) is a circular Kofun located in {{ill|Toki, Gifu|en|Toki, Gifu|qid=Q847332|lt=Toki|1=Toki, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as part of the national [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|Historic Sites of Japan]] under the name "Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun" (乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Danjirimaki Kofun (left) and [[Otozuka Kofun]] (right in the background)|qq=}}]] Danjirimaki Kofun is located on the northern bank of the {{ill|Shōnai River|ja|土岐川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川|1=Shōnai River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7506182}}, at the edge of a fluvial terrace approximately 150 meters above sea level in southeastern Gifu Prefecture. Excavations were conducted starting in fiscal year 2002. The mound is circular, with a diameter of 23.9 meters and a remaining height of 4.1 meters, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in Toki City. The mound lacks a surrounding ditch and any evidence of stepped construction or surface stone lining. The burial chamber is a pseudo-double-sleeved {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} oriented toward the south-southeast. The tumulus is dated to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, corresponding to the terminal period of the [[Kofun period]]. It is considered to have been constructed at around the same time as the nearby [[Otozuka Kofun]]. Together, these tombs provide significant insights into the reorganization and control of the Higashi-Mino region by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during this period. The site was Historic Site1 as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938, with additional areas included in 2017. The tumulus has been preserved and opened to the public, although access to the stone chamber is restricted, with limited monthly openings on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Danjirimaki Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s-eye view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Danjirimaki Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] The burial facility consists of a pseudo-double-sleeved {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southeast. Its area are as follows[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Official Website).: * '''Total length''': 9.5 meters * '''Main chamber''': Length 3.6 meters, width 1.6–1.75 meters, height 2.1–2.3 meters * '''Entrance corridor''': Length 3.2 meters, width 1.3–1.4 meters, height 2.1 meters * '''Antechamber''': Length 2.4 meters, width 1.5–1.85 meters The stone chamber was constructed primarily using locally quarried {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}, with chert used for gap-filling stones and the pebble floor. The rear wall is made from a single large stone topped with smaller stones, while the side walls are built with 3–5 tiers of stone blocks. The main entrance features lintel stones supported by two upright pillars. A gravel bed was discovered in the main chamber, although it has been reconstructed approximately 50 cm above its original level. The ceiling comprises three stones in the main chamber and two in the entrance corridor. burial goods recovered include fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, including long-necked jars that suggest connections with the Kinai region. Danjirimaki Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (looking toward the rear wall) Danjirimaki Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (looking toward the entrance) Danjirimaki Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (looking outward) Danjirimaki Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance corridor (looking inward) Danjirimaki Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance of the chamber Danjirimaki 2.jpg|Collapsed stones from the antechamber == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * '''Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun''' - Designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] on December 14, 1938, with an extension of the Historic Site1 area on October 13, 2017{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}. == Related Facilities == * [[Toki City Museum of Mino Ceramics]] (Toki City, Izumicho Kujiri) == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun - Development Plan (Basic Concept and Plan)|publisher=Toki City Board of Education|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun - 5th and 6th Excavation Reports|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/f271032c752d4842ec2a5c3877ba5158.pdf|publisher=Toki City Board of Education|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2023|title=Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun - 7th to 9th Excavation Reports|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/da4c5f8fd60c22f48879920e8f51d2e5.pdf|publisher=Toki City Board of Education|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=齊藤基生|year=1989|title=Japanese Encyclopedia of Ancient Tombs|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Otozuka Kofun|ja|乙塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otozuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun with Danjirimaki Kofun"] - Toki City Official Website {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|段尻巻古墳|version=101172352|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:段尻巻古墳]] {{Commons category|Danjirimaki Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q123116852}} [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
